title: Deep Learning Practicum (Three Assignments)
summary: A three-assignment deep learning practicum. Assignment 1 builds a multi-layer perceptron from scratch in NumPy and PyTorch on CIFAR-10. Assignment 2 Part 2 implements CLIP-based zero-shot evaluation and learnable visual prompting. Assignment 3 implements a Variational Autoencoder (VAE) and an Adversarial Autoencoder (AAE) together with their training loops.
instructionPreview: This is a Deep Learning 1 practicum-style sequence. It covers three assignments: MLP backpropagation in NumPy, CLIP-based visual prompting in PyTorch, and deep generative models (VAE and Adversarial Autoencoder). The student-written code has been removed and you must re-implement entire subsystems so that all tests pass.
instruction:
This is a Deep Learning 1 practicum-style sequence. It covers three assignments: MLP backpropagation in NumPy, CLIP-based visual prompting in PyTorch, and deep generative models (VAE and Adversarial Autoencoder). The student-written code has been removed and you must re-implement entire subsystems so that all tests pass.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. All project code is already present under subdirectories `assignment1/`, `assignment2/part2/`, `assignment3/part1/`, and `assignment3/part2/`.

## Modules and Scripts to Implement

The following modules need to be implemented or substantially built out:

- **NumPy NN Modules** (`assignment1/modules.py`): Core neural network building blocks in NumPy — Kaiming-initialized linear layer setup, cache management, ELU activation, softmax, and cross-entropy loss. Each module needs its student TODO regions restored. (~4 classes, ~12 methods/blocks to implement)

- **NumPy MLP** (`assignment1/mlp_numpy.py`): Multi-layer perceptron built on top of the NumPy modules. Needs network initialization, forward pass, and backward pass. (~1 class, ~3 functions to implement)

- **PyTorch MLP** (`assignment1/mlp_pytorch.py`): Multi-layer perceptron using PyTorch nn.Module, with optional batch normalization and Kaiming weight initialization. (~1 class, ~2 functions to implement)

- **NumPy MLP Training** (`assignment1/train_mlp_numpy.py`): Training pipeline for the NumPy MLP on CIFAR-10, including confusion matrix computation, metrics extraction (accuracy, precision, recall, F1), model evaluation, and the full training loop with SGD. (~4 functions to implement)

- **PyTorch MLP Training** (`assignment1/train_mlp_pytorch.py`): Training pipeline for the PyTorch MLP on CIFAR-10, including confusion matrix computation, metrics extraction, model evaluation, and the full training loop with optimizer and loss module. (~4 functions to implement)

- **PyTorch MLP Comparison Runner** (`assignment1/compare_models.py`): Experiment runner that trains several PyTorch MLP configurations, writes their metrics to disk, and plots train/validation curves for comparison. (~2 functions to implement)

- **Visual Prompters** (`assignment2/part2/vp.py`): Three visual prompting strategies for CLIP-based learning — a border-padding prompter, a fixed-position patch prompter, and a random-position patch prompter. Each needs initialization of learnable parameters and a forward method. (~3 classes, ~6 functions to implement)

- **Zero-Shot CLIP Evaluation** (`assignment2/part2/clipzs.py`): Text feature precomputation, CLIP image inference, and the evaluation loop for zero-shot CIFAR classification. (~1 class, ~3 functions/code blocks to implement)

- **Prompted CLIP Wrapper** (`assignment2/part2/vpt_model.py`): CLIP wrapper that builds text embeddings for class prompts, applies a learnable visual prompt to images, and returns prompt-conditioned logits. (~1 class, ~2 functions to implement)

- **Visual Prompt Learner** (`assignment2/part2/learner.py`): Training and evaluation logic that freezes the CLIP backbone, optimizes only prompt parameters, and measures prompt accuracy. (~1 class, ~3 code blocks to implement)

- **Cross-Dataset Prompt Evaluation** (`assignment2/part2/cross_dataset.py`): Evaluation script that combines CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 label vocabularies, reuses a trained prompt model, and reports weighted combined accuracy. (~1 script, ~4 code blocks to implement)

- **VAE Utilities** (`assignment3/part1/utils.py`): Utility functions for the Variational Autoencoder — reparameterization trick sampling, KL divergence computation, and ELBO-to-bits-per-dimension conversion. (~3 functions to implement)

- **VAE CNN Encoder/Decoder** (`assignment3/part1/cnn_encoder_decoder.py`): CNN-based encoder and decoder for the VAE. The encoder maps images to a mean and log-std latent distribution. The decoder maps latent codes back to image logits. Both need architecture initialization and forward methods. (~2 classes, ~4 functions to implement)

- **VAE Training Module** (`assignment3/part1/train_pl.py`): PyTorch Lightning VAE module that ties encoder, decoder, and loss functions together. Needs the forward method (encode, reparameterize, decode, compute losses) and the sampling method. (~1 class, ~2 functions to implement)

- **Adversarial Autoencoder** (`assignment3/part2/models.py`): Full adversarial autoencoder with convolutional encoder, convolutional decoder (with Tanh output), and MLP discriminator. Needs architecture initialization for all three components, forward methods, autoencoder loss computation (reconstruction + generator), discriminator loss computation, and sampling. (~4 classes, ~10 functions to implement)

- **AAE Training Loop** (`assignment3/part2/train.py`): End-to-end training script for the adversarial autoencoder, including the autoencoder/discriminator update steps and optimizer construction. (~2 functions, ~3 code blocks to implement)

## Test-Coupled Signatures

The following functions are called directly by the test suite. You must implement them with these exact signatures:

- `LinearModule.forward(self, x) -> np.ndarray` — compute linear transformation and cache input
- `LinearModule.backward(self, dout) -> np.ndarray` — compute input gradient and store weight/bias gradients
- `ELUModule.forward(self, x) -> np.ndarray` — apply ELU activation element-wise
- `ELUModule.backward(self, dout) -> np.ndarray` — compute ELU gradient
- `SoftMaxModule.forward(self, x) -> np.ndarray` — compute numerically-stable softmax
- `SoftMaxModule.backward(self, dout) -> np.ndarray` — backpropagate through softmax
- `CrossEntropyModule.forward(self, x, y) -> float` — compute mean cross-entropy loss
- `CrossEntropyModule.backward(self, x, y) -> np.ndarray` — compute loss gradient w.r.t. x
- `PadPrompter.__init__(self, args)` — initialize learnable border-padding parameters
- `PadPrompter.forward(self, x) -> torch.Tensor` — add border prompt to image, return same shape
- `FixedPatchPrompter.__init__(self, args)` — initialize learnable patch parameter
- `FixedPatchPrompter.forward(self, x) -> torch.Tensor` — overlay patch at top-left corner
- `RandomPatchPrompter.__init__(self, args)` — initialize learnable patch parameter
- `RandomPatchPrompter.forward(self, x) -> torch.Tensor` — overlay patch at random position
- `sample_reparameterize(mean, std) -> Tensor` — sample using reparameterization trick
- `KLD(mean, log_std) -> Tensor` — KL divergence from standard normal, summed over last dim
- `elbo_to_bpd(elbo, img_shape) -> Tensor` — convert ELBO to bits-per-dimension
- `ConvEncoder.forward(self, x) -> Tensor` — encode input to latent codes [B, z_dim]
- `ConvDecoder.forward(self, z) -> Tensor` — decode latent to images [B, 1, 28, 28]
- `Discriminator.forward(self, z) -> Tensor` — predict real/fake for latent codes [B, 1]
- `AdversarialAE.forward(self, x) -> (Tensor, Tensor)` — encode then decode, return (recon, z)
- `AdversarialAE.get_loss_autoencoder(self, x, recon_x, z_fake, lambda_=1) -> (Tensor, dict)` — compute autoencoder loss combining MSE reconstruction and generator adversarial loss
- `AdversarialAE.get_loss_discriminator(self, z_fake) -> (Tensor, dict)` — compute discriminator loss for real (sampled from prior) and fake (encoded) latent codes
- `AdversarialAE.sample(self, batch_size) -> Tensor` — generate images by decoding random latent codes from the standard normal prior
- `MLP.__init__(self, n_inputs, n_hidden, n_classes)` — initialize NumPy MLP with linear layers and activations (assignment1/mlp_numpy.py)
- `MLP.forward(self, x) -> np.ndarray` — flatten input and pass through all layers (assignment1/mlp_numpy.py)
- `MLP.backward(self, dout)` — backpropagate gradients through all layers in reverse (assignment1/mlp_numpy.py)
- `MLP.__init__(self, n_inputs, n_hidden, n_classes, use_batch_norm=False)` — initialize PyTorch MLP with nn.Module layers and Kaiming init (assignment1/mlp_pytorch.py)
- `MLP.forward(self, x) -> Tensor` — flatten input and pass through all layers (assignment1/mlp_pytorch.py)
- `confusion_matrix(predictions, targets) -> np.ndarray` — compute N-class confusion matrix from model predictions and true labels
- `confusion_matrix_to_metrics(confusion_matrix, beta=1.) -> dict` — compute accuracy, precision, recall, F1-beta from confusion matrix
- `CNNEncoder.__init__(self, num_input_channels, num_filters, z_dim)` — build CNN encoder architecture for VAE (assignment3/part1)
- `CNNEncoder.forward(self, x) -> (Tensor, Tensor)` — encode images to (mean, log_std) tensors
- `CNNDecoder.__init__(self, num_input_channels, num_filters, z_dim)` — build CNN decoder architecture for VAE (assignment3/part1)
- `CNNDecoder.forward(self, z) -> Tensor` — decode latent codes to image logits

## Additional Student Entry Points

The following non-test-specific entry points are also hollowed and need to be rebuilt:

- `train_models(results_filename)` and `plot_results(results_filename)` in `assignment1/compare_models.py`
- `ZeroshotCLIP.precompute_text_features(self, clip_model, prompts, device) -> Tensor`
- `ZeroshotCLIP.model_inference(self, image) -> Tensor`
- `CustomCLIP.forward(self, image) -> Tensor`
- `Learner.train_one_epoch(self, epoch)` and `Learner.evaluate(self, split="valid")`
- `train_aae(epoch, model, train_loader, logger_ae, logger_disc, optimizer_ae, optimizer_disc, lambda_=1)`

moduleDag.description: A three-assignment deep learning practicum. Assignment 1 builds a multi-layer perceptron from scratch in NumPy and PyTorch on CIFAR-10. Assignment 2 Part 2 implements CLIP-based zero-shot evaluation and learnable visual prompting. Assignment 3 implements a Variational Autoencoder (VAE) and an Adversarial Autoencoder (AAE) together with their training loops.

nodes.label: NumPy Neural Network Modules
nodes.description: Core building blocks for a hand-coded neural network in NumPy: LinearModule setup with Kaiming initialisation and cache management, ELUModule, SoftMaxModule (numerically stable), and CrossEntropyModule. These are the primitives used by the NumPy MLP.

nodes.label: PyTorch MLP
nodes.description: Multi-layer perceptron using nn.Module and nn.ModuleList, with optional BatchNorm and Kaiming weight initialisation. Completely independent of the NumPy track.

nodes.label: Visual Prompters for CLIP
nodes.description: Three learnable visual-prompting strategies for CLIP: PadPrompter, FixedPatchPrompter, and RandomPatchPrompter. Each is a standalone nn.Module.

nodes.label: Zero-Shot CLIP Evaluation
nodes.description: Standalone zero-shot CLIP evaluation pipeline that precomputes prompt text features, performs CLIP image inference, and evaluates top-1 accuracy on CIFAR datasets.

nodes.label: VAE Utility Functions
nodes.description: Three self-contained mathematical helpers consumed by the VAE training module: sample_reparameterize, KLD, and elbo_to_bpd.

nodes.label: VAE CNN Encoder & Decoder
nodes.description: CNN-based recognition (encoder) and generative (decoder) networks for the VAE. CNNEncoder maps images to (mean, log_std), while CNNDecoder maps latent vectors back to image logits.

nodes.label: Adversarial Autoencoder Models
nodes.description: Encoder, decoder, discriminator, and top-level AdversarialAE module for the AAE. Includes model architectures, losses, and sampling.

nodes.label: NumPy MLP
nodes.description: Multi-layer perceptron assembled from the NumPy modules. Implements forward and backward over a stack of LinearModule, ELUModule, and SoftMaxModule layers.

nodes.label: PyTorch MLP Training Pipeline
nodes.description: CIFAR-10 training loop for the PyTorch MLP using SGD and nn.CrossEntropyLoss, including confusion-matrix metrics and validation checkpointing.

nodes.label: Prompted CLIP Wrapper
nodes.description: CLIP wrapper that precomputes prompt text embeddings, applies a learnable visual prompt to images, and returns prompt-conditioned similarity logits.

nodes.label: VAE PyTorch Lightning Training Module
nodes.description: PyTorch Lightning module that wires CNNEncoder, CNNDecoder, and the VAE utility functions into a complete training step and sampling path.

nodes.label: AAE Training Loop
nodes.description: End-to-end training script for the adversarial autoencoder, including alternating autoencoder and discriminator updates plus optimizer construction.

nodes.label: NumPy MLP Training Pipeline
nodes.description: CIFAR-10 training loop for the NumPy MLP with manual SGD updates, confusion-matrix metrics, validation selection, and final test evaluation.

nodes.label: PyTorch MLP Comparison Runner
nodes.description: Script for running several PyTorch MLP hyperparameter configurations, persisting the collected results, and plotting train/validation curves for comparison.

nodes.label: Visual Prompt Learner
nodes.description: Trainer for prompt learning with CLIP that freezes the backbone, updates only the prompt parameters, and evaluates prompt accuracy on validation/test sets.

nodes.label: Cross-Dataset Prompt Evaluation
nodes.description: Evaluation script that merges CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 prompt vocabularies, updates the CLIP text features accordingly, and computes weighted combined accuracy.

edges.label: mlp_numpy.py imports and instantiates LinearModule, ELUModule, and SoftMaxModule from modules.py
train_mlp_numpy.py instantiates MLP and calls forward/backward during manual SGD training
train_mlp_pytorch.py instantiates the PyTorch MLP and optimizes it on CIFAR-10
compare_models.py reuses the train() entry point from train_mlp_pytorch.py for each experiment configuration
vpt_model.py imports the prompt modules into PROMPT_TYPES and instantiates one as self.prompt_learner
learner.py builds CustomCLIP as self.vpt and drives it through training and evaluation
cross_dataset.py loads Learner, reuses its prompted CLIP model, and swaps in merged text features for evaluation
train_pl.py calls sample_reparameterize, KLD, and elbo_to_bpd inside the VAE forward path
train_pl.py instantiates CNNEncoder and CNNDecoder and calls them during forward and sample
train.py instantiates AdversarialAE and calls its forward, loss, and sample methods during optimization
